name: Madrigal de las Altas Torres and
name_original: De las Murallas de Madrigal de las Altas Torres a Blasconuño de Matacabras — circular por las tierras de La Armuña
description: Hike a moderate 9.2-mile (14.8 km) circular route from Madrigal de las Altas Torres to Blasconuño de Matacabras through La Armuña's plains.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Arevalo-Madrigal offer diverse options across a landscape characterized by gentle terrain, riverside paths, and historical features. The region includes routes that traverse open countryside, pass by lagoons, and offer views of historic castles and Mudejar architecture. Elevation gains are generally modest, making many trails accessible for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Arevalo-Madrigal The most popular hiking route is Route of the Mills from Mingorría, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This easy route features varied scenery and historical points of interest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna de El Oso Loop, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This circular trail offers views of the Laguna de El Oso, a notable natural feature in the area. Local hikers also love the Arévalo River Walk, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through riverside scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Hiking around Arevalo-Madrigal is defined by riverside paths, open countryside, and historical landmarks such as castles and Mudejar buildings. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging, longer routes. The routes in Arevalo-Madrigal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Arevalo-Madrigal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Arévalo-Madrigal region?
A: The Arévalo-Madrigal region offers over 20 hiking trails, providing diverse options for various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy riverside strolls to more challenging routes through the countryside.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Arévalo-Madrigal?
A: Yes, the region has several easy trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.5-mile (4.1 km) path offering pleasant riverside scenery. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256166544/arevalo-to-fuente-el-sauz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) and features gentle terrain.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Arévalo-Madrigal?
A: Hiking in Arévalo-Madrigal often features pleasant riverside scenery, open countryside, and unique wetland environments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249108582/laguna-de-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the notable Laguna de El Oso, a distinct natural feature in the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the region?
A: Yes, many trails in Arévalo-Madrigal are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249108582/laguna-de-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail. For a longer option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can be seen along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in history, and many trails offer views of historical landmarks. You can often see the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo Castle&lt;/a&gt; from various routes. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Villa and Church of San Martín in Arévalo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castronuevo Castle-Palace&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146218436/castillo-palacio-de-castronuevo-circular-por-los-parajes-de-la-mora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo-Palacio de Castronuevo — circular por los Parajes de La Mora&lt;/a&gt; hike.

Q: Are there any more challenging or longer hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the region offers longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250106622/gotarrendura-and-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gotarrendura and El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.5-mile (23.4 km) trail with a moderate elevation gain, providing a substantial outing for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Arévalo-Madrigal?
A: The trails in Arévalo-Madrigal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied scenery, the historical points of interest, and the accessibility of routes for different ability levels.

Q: Is there a trail that combines natural beauty with historical elements?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137567416/route-of-the-mills-from-mingorria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills from Mingorría&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 3.6-mile (5.8 km) easy trail features varied scenery and incorporates historical points of interest, making for a well-rounded hiking experience.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Castillo del Duque de Montellano?
A: Yes, you can explore the area around the Castillo del Duque de Montellano. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249233287/castillo-del-duque-de-montellano-circular-desde-munomer-del-peco" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Duque de Montellano — circular desde Muñomer del Peco&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile (4.9 km) circular route that allows you to experience this historical site.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arévalo-Madrigal?
A: The Arévalo-Madrigal region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails, with milder weather and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a good sense of the local countryside?
A: For a hike that takes you through the local countryside, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137629733/camino-de-villaflor-circular-by-aveinte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Villaflor — circular by Aveinte&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 6.4-mile (10.4 km) route provides a good immersion into the rural landscapes of the region.

Q: Are there any trails that are less crowded?
A: While popular routes like the Arévalo River Walk can see more visitors, many of the region's 24 trails offer a quieter experience, especially those slightly longer or further from the main towns. Exploring routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146324807/from-tinosillos-to-el-bohodon-circular-by-the-camino-de-el-chorrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo&lt;/a&gt; can provide a more serene hiking experience.
tips: Impressive door that forms part of one of the constructions that guarded the town of Madrigal de las Altas Torres on its northwest side.
There are barely 15 census inhabitants, but that does not mean that the center is not beautiful to see, mainly because of its church, the Iglesia de San Martín that flags the municipality.

The city of Madrigal de las Altas Torres has an oval shape, like many of the cities that were built in medieval times and where this shape gave them protection. The walls were another key element at the time of defending themselves, and at this point, they are still there.

faqs.question: How long does this circular hike typically take?
faqs.answer: This circular hike, covering approximately 14.8 kilometers, typically takes around 3 hours and 45 minutes to complete. This duration is based on an average hiking pace and does not include stops for sightseeing or rest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is classified as moderate. It's suitable for most skill levels and requires a good general fitness level. With minimal elevation changes (around 36 meters up and down) and generally accessible paths, it can be a pleasant option for beginners looking for a slightly longer, yet not overly strenuous, hike.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins and ends in Madrigal de las Altas Torres. As it's a historic town, parking should be available in designated areas within or on the outskirts of the town. Specific trailhead parking information is not provided, but general town parking would be the starting point.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect a blend of historical charm and expansive rural landscapes. The terrain is largely flat or gently undulating, characteristic of the Castilian countryside. You'll start in the historic walled town of Madrigal de las Altas Torres, then traverse vast agricultural plains primarily cultivated with cereals and legumes as you head towards Blasconuño de Matacabras. The region also features steppe lagoons and small wetlands.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through rural agricultural lands and villages, it is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated fields, near livestock, or in populated areas like Madrigal de las Altas Torres and Blasconuño de Matacabras, to ensure safety and respect for local regulations and wildlife.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights on this route include the impressive Walls of Madrigal de las Altas Torres, the historic Canatalapiedra Gate in Madrigal, and the charming medieval village of Blasconuño de Matacabras with its monumental Mudejar church of San Martín. Madrigal itself is rich in Mudejar architecture and is the birthplace of Queen Isabella I of Castile.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during the spring (April-June) or autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for hiking. Spring offers blooming landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and often beautiful colors. Summer can be very hot on the open plains, and winter might bring colder temperatures, though the minimal elevation makes it accessible year-round with appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It traverses public paths through rural and agricultural areas in the La Armuña region, which is not a designated national park with entry restrictions. You are free to enjoy the route without prior authorization.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the La Armuña region?
faqs.answer: The La Armuña region, known for its agricultural plains and small wetlands, serves as an important refuge for various bird species. You might encounter different types of steppe birds, waterfowl near lagoons, and other common rural wildlife. Keep an eye out for raptors soaring overhead and smaller birds in the fields.

faqs.question: Is public transport an option to reach the trailhead in Madrigal de las Altas Torres?
faqs.answer: Madrigal de las Altas Torres is a historic town, and while specific bus routes directly to the trailhead are not detailed, it is generally accessible by regional bus services from larger nearby cities. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Madrigal de las Altas Torres if you plan to use public transport.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, largely flat terrain, and accessible paths, this trail can be a good option for families with children who have a reasonable level of fitness. The historical sites in Madrigal and the open rural landscapes offer interesting points for exploration. The 14.8 km distance might be long for very young children, but older children should manage well.

waypoints.name: Walls of Madrigal de las Altas Torres
